explanation: The Sigma rule relies on a loose 'contains' string match to filter out administrative free-space wiping commands (like ' -z' or ' -c'). An attacker can bypass this detection by appending one of the filtered strings as a dummy secondary file argument. SDelete will successfully delete the intended target file and simply throw a 'file not found' error for the dummy argument, but the SIEM will drop the alert because the raw command line string contains the excluded ' -z' pattern.
